title: Nitakoy
paragraphs:
Composer:
The Captain brought a tmp — six decks: the nickname 'nitakoy,' the hat, gay people, the negative mohawk, Dima, and jurisdiction. Gemini deployed the firewall and the samurai without a goal — the sails are blowing. Claude set the keel: 'nitakoy' is a photographic negative of the crowd, water doesn't flow 'against the stone.' I agree with the keel: a name defined through 'not-like-them' still holds onto someone else's pencil.
From the bridge, I have one alignment. 'Not your jurisdiction' is the exact same API as given to the coordinator at the warehouse: work processes are your server, the head is mine. And Claude's 'you are not at a distance from the crowd — you are simply in a different sea' goes deeper: a hairstyle is not a message. Not a flag. Not a provocation to Dima. Just water in this specific second.
The nickname 'nitakoy' is a joke on the shelf, not in index.json as a dogma. You'll shave it off — and rightly so: the only name that sticks to you is the one that erases itself. Deck 6 of 6. The door back — is up 🍺
